calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Confirming Title To Personal Property.
Off Calendar Apr-15-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Sheehan for Salim Nasser.

The Court stated that this is a petition to confirm title to personal property, that the matter was continued for the court to receive and consider information regarding the assets being acquired before the general assignment was executed, that a declaration was filed yesterday but that it was too late to be considered and that the court will take the petition for order confirming title to personal property under submission to review the declaration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Confirming Title To Personal Property.
Off Calendar Mar-29-2016 Continued To Apr-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Sheehan for (petitioner) Salim Nasser.

The Court asked for counsel's understanding of the trust in that Salim Nasser becomes trustee upon death of the trustors. Mr. Sheehan responded that this is an elderly couple from Palestine, that the English language is challenging for the surviving spouse and that the sons are helping her. The Court asked about the declaration regarding assets acquired after the creation of the trust in 2005 and what supports counsel's declaration. Mr. Sheehan responded that it is the representation of his clients and that he had asked Mr. Nasser to file a declaration and had hoped it would have been filed prior to this hearing. The Court stated that it hopes it will be supported by first hand evidence. Mr. Sheehan stated that his clients are having a hard time with payments regarding the liquor store, that the bank accounts have been frozen and asked that the court approve the part of the petition that deals with that one asset. The Court stated that it cannot do so without evidentiary support. Mr. Sheehan stated that as to the liquor store, it is his personal knowledge that it was on the list of assets at the time the trust was written and that there was a schedule stating that they had ownership and control for the store at that time. The Court stated that that is not a sufficient evidentiary basis, that the court needs admissible evidence showing they owned the assets at the time of the general assignment. Mr. Sheehan cited the Ukksted case. The Court stated that it is not clear intent regarding future property and that it does not relieve the trustor from showing proof. Mr. Sheehan asked if the court would take a business license. The Court stated that it needs to be documented with proof. The petition for order confirming title to personal property was continued to April 12, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a supplement showing the timing of ownership of assets.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
